assertions.description: window.AnimationEvent exists — the event interface the read-only animation attribute is added to.
window.TransitionEvent exists — the companion event interface getting the same attribute.
window.CSSAnimation exists — the Animation subclass returned for CSS-generated animations.
window.CSSTransition exists — the Animation subclass returned for CSS-generated transitions.

'animation' is a member of AnimationEvent.prototype — the new read-only INSTANCE attribute returning the CSSAnimation? that fired the event (Chrome 151 per the milestone listing; BCD also records Firefox 152 / Safari 27).
'animation' is a member of TransitionEvent.prototype — the companion INSTANCE attribute returning the CSSTransition? that fired the event.

The accessor is an instance attribute, never a static on the constructors: neither AnimationEvent.animation nor TransitionEvent.animation exists as a constructor property (this holds whether or not the accessor is implemented).
assertions.test: (()=>{ const a = typeof AnimationEvent === 'undefined' || typeof AnimationEvent.animation === 'undefined'; const t = typeof TransitionEvent === 'undefined' || typeof TransitionEvent.animation === 'undefined'; return a && t; })()

assertions.description: The AnimationEvent attribute is nullable: when the accessor exists, a synthetic event constructed without the init member reports animation === null (not undefined or a fabricated Animation).
assertions.test: (()=>{ if (typeof AnimationEvent === 'undefined' || !('animation' in AnimationEvent.prototype)) return false; const e = new AnimationEvent('animationstart'); return e.animation === null; })()

assertions.description: The TransitionEvent attribute is likewise nullable: when the accessor exists, a synthetic event constructed without the init member reports animation === null.
assertions.test: (()=>{ if (typeof TransitionEvent === 'undefined' || !('animation' in TransitionEvent.prototype)) return false; const e = new TransitionEvent('transitionrun'); return e.animation === null; })()
